title: Oseon 2795
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Oseon}} {{CelestialBody |image= |name=Oseon 2795 |region=*[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]] *[[The Slice/Legends|The Slice]] |sector=[[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] |system=[[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]]''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'' |coordinates=T-8 |xyz= |distance= |suns=[[Oseon sun]] |orbited= |position= |moons= |routes=*[[Cadma Conduit]] *[[Falko Run]] |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class= |diameter= |atmosphere= |climate=[[Temperature/Legends|Hot]] |gravity=[[Gravity/Legends|Minimal]] |terrain= |water= |interest= |flora= |fauna= |otherlife= |species= |otherspecies= |socialgroup= |language= |government= |population= |demonym= |cities= |imports= |exports= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|''[…]'' on Oseon 2795 everybody's down on his luck ''[…]''|Governor Duttes Mer|}} '''Oseon 2795''' was a [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] located in the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. Situated close to its [[Oseon sun|sun]], the asteroid seemed [[Temperature/Legends|hot]] to its occupants, a poor [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] of [[Miner/Legends|miners]], despite the presence of adequate [[Air conditioner|air-conditioning]] and [[Life support/Legends|life support]] systems. In [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], the [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerer of Tund]] [[Rokur Gepta]] and [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Duttes Mer]] of the [[Rafa system/Legends|Rafa system]] sent the [[Sentientologist|anthropologist]] [[Osuno Whett/Legends|Osuno Whett]] to Oseon 2795 to find a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] who could be manipulated into retrieving for Gepta and Mer the [[Mindharp of Sharu/Legends|Mindharp]] artifact of the Rafa [[Star system/Legends|system]]. Whett found his mark in the [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]] and [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], who was playing a game of [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] with Whett, local miners [[Vett Fori]] and [[Arun Feb]], and corrupt [[Oseon Peacekeeper]] [[Constable/Legends|Constable]] [[T. Lund Phuna]]. During the game, Whett enticed Calrissian into traveling to the Rafa system, which the gambler did when Phuna attempted to frame him for cheating, forcing the former to flee the Oseon system. ==Description== [[File:Oseon-TEA.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Oseon 2795 was situated in the Oseon system.]] Oseon 2795 was a minimal-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] [[Iron/Legends|iron]]-[[nickel]] [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] and [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]] located in the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]]. It was situated in the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]], a part of [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Along with the rest of the Oseon [[Star system/Legends|system]], Oseon 2795 was connected by the [[Falko Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] to the [[Arleen system/Legends|Arleen system]], while the [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] route known as the [[Cadma Conduit]] linked it to the [[Erilnar system/Legends|Erilnar system]] and the [[Cadma sector/Legends|Cadma sector's]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} [[Dagelin Minor system/Legends|Dagelin Minor]] and [[Simbarc system/Legends|Simbarc systems]]. Oseon 2795 [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] the [[Oseon sun]] closer than most asteroids in the Oseon system. Due to Oseon 2795's proximity to its energetic [[Star/Legends|sun]], its sere, withered surface was bombarded by a constant flux of [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] that seemed to [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], a visitor to the asteroid, to penetrate into its interior. The rooms situated in the inner portions of the asteroid seemed uncomfortably [[Temperature/Legends|hot]] despite [[Air conditioner|air-conditioning]] and [[Life support/Legends|life support]] systems,''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' which were as thoughtful as those of any of the other orbital bodies of the Oseon system. Calrissian found that the heat—the real or imagined nature of which Calrissian was not certain about—seemed to impede the [[Brain/Legends|mental]] processes of his fellow players of the [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] card game. ==History== ===The need for a dupe=== {{Quote|Meanwhile, we set a little trap. In return for the offer of a new job, once his investigations here were finished, the anthropologist went to Oseon 2795 in search of, well, shall we say a suitably gullible individual to do our work for us.|Governor Duttes Mer|}} [[File:RokurGeptaCloseup-NEGTC.png|thumb|right|300px|Rokur Gepta planned to manipulate a spacer found on Oseon 2795 into retrieving the Mindharp artifact for him.]] By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerer of Tund]] [[Rokur Gepta]] became interested in obtaining the legendary [[Mindharp of Sharu/Legends|Mindharp]] artifact, a product of the formerly technologically-advanced [[Sharu/Legends|Sharu]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of the [[Rafa system/Legends|Rafa system]]. Gepta partnered in his efforts to obtain the Mindharp with the system's [[Governor/Legends|governor]], [[Duttes Mer]], and the pair hired the [[Sentientologist|anthropologist]] ''[[Ottdefa/Legends|Ottdefa]]'' [[Osuno Whett/Legends|Osuno Whett]] to investigate the Mindharp-related legends of the [[Toka]], the primitive remnants of the Sharu civilization. Once Whett's investigations were complete, Gepta and Mer, aware that the Toka folklore prophesied that the Mindharp would be discovered by a [[Key Bearer|stranger]] to the Toka who was a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] accompanied by a [[Color/Legends|silver]]-[[Armor/Legends|armored]] non-[[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Emissary (mythology)|being]], dispatched the ''Ottdefa'' to Oseon 2795 in return for the offer of a new job. On the asteroid, where Gepta and Mer had the cooperation of the [[Oseon Peacekeeper|local law enforcement]] personnel, Whett was to find an unlucky [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]]. Whett then had to entice the spacer with the ownership of his valuable [[Class Two multi-phasic robot|droid]], [[Vuffi Raa]], as well as stories about the [[Treasure of Rafa]]—a collection of material valuables that was supposedly stored inside the abandoned monumental structures left behind in the Rafa system by the disappeared Sharu culture—into traveling to the Rafa system so that Gepta and Mer could then use the unwitting individual in order to gain access to the Mindharp. Whett carried out his mission to Oseon 2795 under the pretense—as confirmed by his entry documents—of studying the life of the poor local miners in contrast to the rest of the wealthy Oseon system. The ''Ottdefa''{{'s}} presence on the asteroid bewildered [[Vett Fori]], [[Supervisor/Legends|chief supervisor]] of the local [[Mining/Legends|mining]] operation, since the miners had not hired an anthropologist. Instead, Whett's studies were supposedly financed by an Imperial grant. ===A game of chance=== {{Quote|Ah … not exactly the Imperial Crown jewels, nor even the fabulous Treasure of Rafa, but—|''Ottdefa'' Osuno Whett, on his sabacc winnings|}} [[File:Young_Lando-TEA.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Lando Calrissian entered a game of sabacc on Oseon 2795.]] Around the same time, in [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] trilogy, of which ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'' is the first installment, to between [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. Lando Calrissian, a [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]] and the captain of the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], planned to travel to the [[Dela system/Legends|Dela system]] in order to play cards. Being only a novice [[Starship/Legends|ship]]-handler, however, he became intimidated by the prospects of both the complicated route to the system and the reputedly tricky [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] landing that had to be performed there. Instead, Calrissian took advice from a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] attendant and traveled to Oseon 2795, although he encountered landing difficulties at the asteroid as well.[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] On Oseon 2795, Calrissian entered a game of sabacc with Whett; Fori; [[Arun Feb]], Fori's [[Aide/Legends|assistant]]; and corrupt [[Constable/Legends|Constable]] [[T. Lund Phuna]] of the Oseon Peacekeepers,''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'' who had been tasked by Gepta and Mer to cause a situation where Calrissian had to flee the system—preferably for the Rafa system. Due to the heat experienced by the beings in the small room, the local players soon stripped to [[Clothing/Legends|shorts]] and [[shirt]]-sleeves and the majority of the quintet were [[Diet/Legends|drinking]] [[Water/Legends|ice water]]. Two [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] into the game, Calrissian had had little luck, and his meager winnings made him muse that he would be forced to drain the charge from his [[electric shaver]] into the [[Energy storage unit|energy storage system]] of the ''Millennium Falcon'' just so that the [[Light freighter/Legends|ship]] could lift off Oseon 2795, of which the gambler thought as a "[[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]]-forsaken" planetoid. ===The trap=== {{Quote|The ''Ottdefa'' here can post bond to you in the full amount of—say, what's ''this''? A cheater! He had a cheater all the time! He could change the faces of the cards to suit him any time he wanted! No wonder—|Constable T. Lund Phuna, to Lando Calrissian|}} At one point during the game, Whett name-dropped the Treasure of Rafa, leading to a conversation about it among the players. As a result, Calrissian began to consider traveling to the Rafa system. The prospect of the relatively easy journey there, coupled with the allure of the idea of the treasure, re-kindled Calrissian's enthusiasm for the ongoing card game, with the asteroid's heat intruding less on his senses as a result. [[File:VuffiNew.png|thumb|right|300px|Lando Calrissian won the droid Vuffi Raa in the Oseon 2795 sabacc game.]] Mindful of his prior troubles with landing on the asteroid and realizing he could use a [[Pilot droid/Legends|pilot droid]] to help travel to and perform a planetary landing in the Rafa system, Calrissian then allowed Whett to bet his [[Droid/Legends|droid]] on the game. When Calrissian won the sabacc hand and Whett revealed that, instead of being on Oseon 2795, the droid was, in fact, located on the planet [[Rafa IV]], the gambler became angry and considered using his concealed [[Stingbeam/Legends|pistol]] on the anthropologist before realizing that—given that the laws of the Oseon system forbade carrying lethal weapons—he could end up [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] or [[Death/Legends|killed]] on the asteroid as a result. At that moment, Phuna planted a [[cheater]] device on Calrissian and then pretended to discover it. The enraged Whett used Oseon 2795's negligible gravity to lunge at the gambler but was knocked out by Feb, who had noticed Phuna planting the cheater. Trusting her assistant's judgment, Fori then rendered Phuna unconscious as well and instructed Calrissian to take his winnings and Whett's documents concerning his droid and leave the Oseon system. Within twenty [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] of leaving the room, Calrissian rented—in return for a substantial deposit—and bolted down in the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] of his ship a [[Class five droid/Legends|Class Five]] [[Unidentified pilot droid (Oseon 2795)|pilot droid]], and ten minutes later, he departed the Oseon system for the Rafa system. [[Standard month/Legends|Months]] later, after Calrissian had indeed been manipulated by Gepta and Mer into retrieving the Mindharp, Mer, while gloating over his capture of the captain, explained to him how the trap at Oseon 2795 had been set up. Calrissian's visit to the asteroid was eventually recorded in a [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] history [[The Essential Chronology|chronicle]] by the [[Historical Council]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. ==Inhabitants== {{Quote|What use have I for worldly wealth? ''[…]"
"''Well, you surely came to the right place, then! No worldly wealth to get in your way at all!|''Ottdefa'' Osuno Whett and Chief Supervisor Vett Fori|}} Unlike the rest of the wealthy asteroid settlements of the Oseon system, Oseon 2795 was a poor [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] of primarily hardrock [[Miner/Legends|miners]], with the Rafa system's governor Duttes Mer characterizing the entirety of the asteroid's population as being unlucky. According to Lando Calrissian's observations, all the miners of Oseon 2795 were of small height, with the gambler speculating that compactness of the body was a virtue among the local population. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:TheMindharpofSharu.png|thumb|right|200px|Oseon 2795 was introduced in ''Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu''.]] Oseon 2795 served as the opening setting for ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'', the [[1983]] first entry in [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] trilogy of novels by [[L. Neil Smith]]. "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, placed the Oseon system, and therefore Oseon 2795, in the [[Zebitrope sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}} it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.{{UnlicensedStatus}}The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently overrode the ''Voyages SF'' 13 placement by establishing that the Oseon system was instead situated in grid square T-8 as part of the Centrality. ==Appearances== *''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *{{FactFile|126|VUF 1-2|Vuffi Raa}} *{{CSWECite|I||Fori, Vett}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Oseon 2795 |nl=Oseon 2795 }} [[Category:Mining asteroids]] [[Category:Oseon system locations]] [[Category:Planetoids in the Outer Rim Territories]] [[Category:Planetoids in the Slice]]
